prompt: Write `reverse(a)` that reverses the array `a` in place and returns it. Use two pointers; do not use the built-in `reverse`.
Write `isPal(s)` that returns `true` if the string `s` reads the same forward and backward, else `false`. Use two pointers from the ends.
Write `pairSum(a, t)` where `a` is sorted ascending. Return `[lo, hi]`, the indices of two entries that sum to `t`, or `null` if none. Use two pointers; do not use a hash map or nested loop.
Write `maxArea(h)` that returns the largest area `(hi - lo) * min(h[lo], h[hi])` over all pairs of walls in `h`. Use two pointers from the ends; do not check all pairs.
Write `sortedSquares(a)` where `a` is sorted ascending and may contain negatives. Return a new array of the squares in ascending order. Use two pointers from the ends; do not sort afterward.
Write `removeDupes(a)` where `a` is sorted ascending. Overwrite `a` in place so its first `k` entries are the distinct values in order, and return `k`. Use a read pointer and a lagging write pointer.

Write `removeElement(a, val)` that removes every occurrence of `val` from `a` in place, keeping the order of the other entries. Return the count `k` of kept entries. Use a read pointer and a lagging write pointer.
Write `moveZeroes(a)` that moves every `0` in `a` to the end while keeping the order of the non-zero entries, mutating `a` in place and returning it. Use a read pointer and a lagging write pointer.

Write `hasCycle(nxt, start)`. The list is an index array where `nxt[i]` is the next index and `-1` is null. Return `true` if following `nxt` from `start` reaches a cycle, else `false`. Use fast and slow pointers; do not use a set or array to mark visited nodes.

Write `middleIndex(nxt, start)`. The list is an index array where `nxt[i]` is the next index and `-1` is null. Return the index of the middle node reached from `start`; for an even-length list return the second of the two middle nodes. Use fast and slow pointers.

Write `threeSum(a)` that returns all unique triples `[x, y, z]` from `a` with `x + y + z === 0`, each triple in ascending order and no duplicate triples. Sort first, then use two pointers; use a numeric comparator when sorting.
